published_prior_art.cite: Kennedy2019JASA + DiazWilliamsHoffmanSchenck2023JASA + YoungHernanRobins2014EM: standard extended-g-formula identification for stochastic, LMTP, and natural-value longitudinal regimes
closest: WenMarcusYoung2023SMMR studies observed-treatment-process intervention distributions, but not an IPSI-LMTP commutator factorization
closest: SarvetWanisYoungHernandezAlejandroStensrud2023Biometrics studies limited-resource IPSIs, but not a necessary-and-sufficient operator zero phase

structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: VanderLaanLuedtkeDiaz2014EM is cited for the natural-value-estimand representation warning, but the bibliography entry instead gives a different optimal-dynamic-treatment-rule paper with mismatched authorship/venue metadata.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: Identification by finite extended-g-formula sums is already the standard content of Kennedy2019JASA, DiazWilliamsHoffmanSchenck2023JASA, and YoungHernanRobins2014EM once the two policy kernels are specified.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The headline promises an observable commutator/non-equivalence object, but Exhibit 9.1 computes only the proposed product C_delta(P), not the two ordered policy means whose difference it is claimed to equal.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The handoff maps SarvetWanisYoungHernandezAlejandroStensrud2023Biometrics to Conjecture 2 as an iff frontier, but the §8 statement is not phrased against that limited-resource IPSI result.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship zero-phase claim uses SarvetWanisYoungHernandezAlejandroStensrud2023Biometrics only as a broad comparator, with no section, equation, page, or quoted open gap tied to the iff boundary.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The iff zero-phase flagship claim is effectively positioned against one named comparator, SarvetWanisYoungHernandezAlejandroStensrud2023Biometrics, without a generic-class promotion.

novelty_flags.label: Overall tier
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship until the proposal makes the ordered-policy commutator a fully defined independent object rather than a product definition plus routine zero-product phase.

novelty_flags.label: Related work
novelty_flags.one_line: VanderLaanLuedtkeDiaz2014EM is used for the claim that distinct causal questions can share a statistical estimand, but the §13 bibliography item is a different targeted-learning paper and does not support that comparison as cited.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The ordered means psi_{I o M} and psi_{M o I} are only described in prose; the LMTP rule M and the 'normalization' are not given as explicit kernels or finite sums.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The factorization cannot be checked as a mathematical statement until Definition 1 gives explicit formulas for both ordered extended-g-formula functionals.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The statement proves a zero set for the defined coefficient C_delta(P), but the prose says 'commutation is equivalent' without assuming or referencing Conjecture 1's equality to the actual policy-mean difference.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: Given Definition 3, the iff zero phase is just the real zero-product rule after removing the nonzero first factor, so the current proof route has fewer than three nontrivial steps beyond definitions and assumptions.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace Definition 1 with explicit finite formulas for psi_{I o M} and psi_{M o I}, including the exact natural-value LMTP kernel and where the odds shift is applied.
Upgrade Exhibit 9.1 to compute both ordered policy means on the same binary law and show their numeric difference equals C_delta(P), not only the product formula.
Anchor the Sarvet and Wen comparisons to concrete sections/equations and rewrite Conjecture 2 as a comparator-facing statement or remove the comparator promise.

To reach flagship tier, make the zero phase non-definitional: state an independently defined commutation condition for the two explicit operators, then derive the product coefficient as a theorem rather than defining the coefficient as the product.

recommended_next_step: Revise definitions and Exhibit 9.1 so the commutator is computed from the two ordered g-formulas, then re-anchor the Sarvet/Wen comparator claims.
